meta.name: Pad Krapow (Thai Holy Basil Stir-Fry)
meta.subtitle: Fast, spicy basil-garlic stir-fry with a savory-sweet sauce—served over jasmine rice with a crispy fried egg.

source.name: SSA Thai Catalog
notes: Canonical pad krapow (ผัดกะเพรา) profile for SSA catalogs.
Includes optional chicken/beef/pork substitutions and basil fallback guidance.

summary: Pad krapow is a classic Thai stir-fry built on a garlic-chili base, a punchy savory sauce, and holy basil stirred in at the end. It’s commonly served over jasmine rice with a crispy fried egg (kai dao).

yield.notes: Serves 4 with rice (or 2–3 if served street-food style with a fried egg each).

notes: Best made fresh. Reheats well for 1–2 days; basil aroma is brightest day-of.
Sauce can be pre-mixed for faster weeknight cooking.

tasteProfile.notes: Holy basil is peppery and clove-like; add at the end so it stays fragrant.

ingredients.label: Ground pork
notes: Traditional. Swap with ground chicken, beef, or turkey (see variations).

ingredients.label: Thai bird's eye chilies, finely chopped (adjust to taste)
notes: For mild: use 1–2 chilies or a milder chili.
For extra heat: add 1–2 more chilies or a pinch of chili flakes.

ingredients.label: Dark soy sauce (or light soy + a pinch of sugar as fallback)
ingredients.notes: Mainly for color and a deeper soy note.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock or water
ingredients.notes: Helps dissolve the sauce and keeps the stir-fry juicy.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch (optional, for glossy sauce)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp; whisk into the stock before adding.

ingredients.label: Holy basil leaves (krapow), loosely packed
notes: If unavailable: Thai basil is the best fallback; sweet basil works but is less authentic.
Add at the very end to preserve aroma.

ingredients.label: Eggs (for crispy fried eggs, optional but classic)
ingredients.notes: Kai dao: crispy edges, runny yolk.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (for frying eggs)
ingredients.notes: Use enough oil to shallow-fry for crispy edges.

ingredients.label: Lime wedges (optional)
ingredients.notes: A squeeze at the end brightens the dish.

steps.title: Prep: basil, aromatics, and sauce mix
instructions: Pick holy basil leaves; discard tough stems.
Mince garlic and chop chilies finely.
In a small bowl, mix oyster sauce, fish sauce, dark soy, sugar, and stock (whisk in cornstarch if using).
checks: Sauce is pre-mixed (stir-fry will move fast).
Basil is ready to add at the end.

steps.title: Start the stir-fry: garlic-chili base
instructions: Heat wok/skillet over high heat until very hot.
Add neutral oil, then add garlic and chilies.
Stir-fry 15–30 seconds until fragrant (do not burn).
checks: Aromatics smell fragrant; garlic is not browned bitterly.

steps.title: Cook the ground pork
instructions: Add ground pork and spread it out so it sears.
Stir-fry, breaking up clumps, until mostly cooked through and lightly browned in spots.
checks: No pink remains in the majority of the meat.

steps.title: Add sauce and reduce to glossy
instructions: Toss and simmer 60–90 seconds until the sauce clings lightly to the meat.
Taste and adjust: a splash more fish sauce for salt/umami, a pinch more sugar for balance.
checks: Sauce is glossy and coats; not watery.

steps.title: Finish with holy basil
instructions: Add holy basil and toss just until wilted and fragrant (10–20 seconds).
checks: Basil is just wilted; still aromatic and bright.

steps.title: Optional: fry crispy eggs (kai dao)
instructions: Heat oil in a small pan over medium-high until shimmering.
Crack in an egg; spoon hot oil over the whites for crisp edges and set whites while keeping yolk runny.
Remove to a plate; repeat for remaining eggs.
checks: Edges are crisp; whites set; yolk is runny (unless you prefer set yolks).

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Spoon pad krapow over hot jasmine rice.
Top with a crispy fried egg if using.
Serve with lime wedges on the side if desired.

sides.label: Nam pla prik (fish sauce + chilies + lime)

pairings.notes: Cucumber cools the heat; nam pla prik boosts salt/acid/heat at the table.

fridge.notes: Reheat gently; add a small splash of water to loosen sauce. Add fresh basil only if you want to refresh aroma.

freezer.notes: Texture is acceptable but basil aroma fades; freeze only if needed.

notes: Cook ground meat to a safe internal temperature (USDA guidance: 160°F/71°C for ground pork/beef; 165°F/74°C for ground poultry).
Use good ventilation—high heat stir-fry can smoke.

adaptations.title: Holy basil substitutions
notes: Fallback: Thai basil (anise note; closer than sweet basil).
Last resort: sweet basil + a pinch of ground clove or allspice to mimic holy basil’s peppery/clove edge.

adaptations.title: Heat control without losing flavor
notes: For milder versions: use fewer chilies and remove seeds; keep garlic quantity for flavor.
Add a little extra lime at serving for brightness if heat is reduced.

ingredient.ground_pork.notes: Use the same amount. Poultry dries faster—avoid overcooking.
Choose 85/15 for better texture; drain excess fat if needed.
Press and crumble tofu; sear until lightly browned before adding sauce.

ingredient.fish_sauce.notes: Season to taste; keep balance with a touch more lime at serving.

notes: Key technique: pre-mix the sauce. Once garlic and chilies hit the oil, the dish finishes in minutes.
If your wok isn’t very hot, cook in smaller batches to avoid steaming.
Street-food style often uses a slightly sweeter sauce and very crispy-edged egg; adjust sugar up by 2–4g if desired.

reviewNotes: All ingredients include id+label and amount.value/unit; optional flags used where appropriate.
All steps include id+title and instructions[]; timers use seconds/minutes per schema.
